scope: event-tracker feature baseline assessment for session consolidation refactor
question: What is the current state of the event-tracker feature for session consolidation refactoring?

directories.purpose: Classifies parser turn data into immutable event entries
Parses session data from message content
CQRS write-side - handles all file I/O for session journals

root_files.purpose: All union literals, sentinels, and core interfaces (321 lines)
Path builders for session directories and files

violations.description: Hook performs file write to events.md in session.idle handler
Hook writes compaction event to events.md
Hook writes assistant_output event to events.md
Tool governance hook writes trajectory events

cqrs_violations.pattern: All hooks import writers from '../features/event-tracker/writers/' and invoke write operations. The hooks are the callers, writers are the implementers.

files_per_session.sample:
## assistant_output

- **Timestamp**: 2026-03-24T18:05:37.662Z
- **Actor**: unknown
- **Title**: Assistant response
- **Summary**: I'll conduct a thorough investigation...

schema.purposeClass: discovery | brainstorming | research | planning | implementation | gatekeeping | tdd | course-correction

files_per_session.pattern: {timestamp} | session={id} | level={level} | actor={actor} | source={source} | message={msg} | details={details}
files_per_session.sample: 2026-03-24T18:05:37.662Z | session=ses_2defa1827ffeSCplN7ICWdA0fz | level=info | actor=N/A | source=N/A | message=turn_complete agent=unknown text_len=140 | details=

requirement_1_one_file_per_session.current: folder per session with 3+ files (events.md, session.json, diagnostics.log, optionally delegation.md, injection.md, synthesis.md)
requirement_1_one_file_per_session.evidence: paths.ts defines getSessionEventsPath, getSessionDiagnosticsPath, getSessionDelegationPath, getSessionInjectionPath, getSessionMetadataPath, getSessionSynthesisPath - all separate files

requirement_2_semantic_session_naming.expected: human-readable semantic names derived from purpose/timestamp
requirement_2_semantic_session_naming.evidence: session.json shows sessionId is the raw OpenCode session ID, no semantic naming mechanism exists in paths.ts or writers/

requirement_3_user_message_capture.current: ONLY assistant_output is captured, userMessageCount is always 0
requirement_3_user_message_capture.expected: both user messages and assistant outputs captured
requirement_3_user_message_capture.evidence: events.md sample shows only ## assistant_output entries. text-complete-handler.ts:52 only writes assistant_output. types.ts defines user_message type but it's never written by any hook.

requirement_4_sub_session_detection_link.expected: automatic detection and linking of parent/child sessions via delegation packets
requirement_4_sub_session_detection_link.evidence: session.json sample shows parentSessionId: null, childSessionIds: []. No code exists to detect sub-session creation or update parent.childSessionIds.

requirement_5_tool_selection_context_int.current: No tool filtering - all tools are allowed
requirement_5_tool_selection_context_int.expected: selective tool inclusion/exclusion for context integrity
requirement_5_tool_selection_context_int.evidence: tool-governance.ts defines HIVEMIND_MANAGED_TOOLS set but only checks if a tool IS managed - no filtering of which tools are allowed in context. isHivemindManagedTool returns boolean but is never used for filtering.

barrel_export.expected: index.ts exporting all public interfaces, types, and key functions
barrel_export.impact: Consumers must import from deep paths (e.g., ../features/event-tracker/writers/events-writer.js) instead of a single surface

cqrs_boundary.write_side: writers/ (base-writer.ts, events-writer.ts, session-writer.ts, diagnostics-writer.ts, index-writer.ts, synthesizer.ts, formatter.ts)
cqrs_boundary.parse_side: parser/ (turn-parser.ts, header-parser.ts, meta-parser.ts, delegation-extractor.ts, splitter.ts, counter.ts, types.ts)
cqrs_boundary.hooks_integration: src/hooks/ (event-handler.ts, compaction-handler.ts, text-complete-handler.ts, runtime-loader/tool-governance.ts) - CQRS violators

blocked_routes: Cannot implement session consolidation without first resolving CQRS violations - hooks performing writes must be refactored to delegate to tools
Sub-session linking requires delegation packet tracking that doesn't currently exist
User message capture requires message.added event handling which is not implemented in any hook
recommended_next_action: Refactor hooks to use tools instead of direct writer calls. Specifically: create hivemind_session_journal tool that wraps events-writer, session-writer, and diagnostics-writer operations. This separates write concerns (tools) from read/intercept concerns (hooks) per CQRS pattern.
key_findings: event-tracker has complete write infrastructure in writers/ but hooks bypass tools layer and call writers directly (CQRS violation)
Session format uses folder-per-session with 3 files; consolidation to single file requires path.ts refactoring
No user message capture - only assistant_output is tracked via text.complete hook
Sub-session linking fields exist in schema but are never populated - delegation packet correlation missing
No barrel export (index.ts) forces deep imports, complicating consumers
